notes: Realistic prompts a student or instructor might send. Assertions are objective checks to verify against the produced .md file and surrounding work directory. Generate synthetic files with `python scripts/generate_eval_fixtures.py --out-dir evals/fixtures`, or replace the file paths under evals/fixtures/ with your own slide decks before running.

evals.name: concept-heavy-zh
evals.prompt: 帮我把这个课件整理一下,我下周要复习。
evals.expected_output: A Chinese study note named concept_heavy_zh_notes.md saved next to the source. Topic-reorganized prose, 3-8 chapters, bilingual term introductions, ending with 复习要点 and 思考题.
assertions: Output file `concept_heavy_zh_notes.md` exists next to the source .pptx
Output contains a top-level `# ` title heading (exactly one)
Output contains a `## 复习要点` section
Output contains a `## 思考题` section
Number of `## ` chapter headings (excluding 复习要点 and 思考题) is between 3 and 8
At least one term is introduced bilingually using the `中文 (English)` pattern
The 思考题 section contains between 5 and 10 numbered items
Output body is predominantly narrative prose (more sentence-ending punctuation `。` than standalone bullet lines starting with `- ` or `• `)
Important or difficult topics are not merely restated from slide bullets; they include intuition plus at least one example, comparison, derivation step, or common pitfall
Every `![...](...)` image reference resolves to an actual file in the `concept_heavy_zh_assets/` directory
`course_memory.json` exists next to the source file
`course_memory.json` contains a lecture record whose `source_file` is `concept_heavy_zh.pptx`

evals.name: math-heavy-en-to-zh
evals.prompt: 这是我们教授的英文 slides,做一份中文笔记吧,公式记得用 LaTeX。
evals.expected_output: A Chinese study note. All formulas that appeared as screenshots in the source PDF have been rewritten as LaTeX. Technical English terms get bilingual introductions on first use.
assertions: Output file `math_heavy_en_notes.md` exists
Output contains at least one block-level LaTeX formula delimited by `$$ ... $$`
Output contains at least three LaTeX expressions total (inline `$...$` or block `$$...$$`)
Important formulas are explained with symbol definitions and the reasoning or derivation step that makes the formula useful
The main body (excluding code blocks and LaTeX) is predominantly Chinese characters
At least two English technical terms get bilingual first-use introduction in the `中文 (English)` form
No image reference in the output points to a file that is purely a formula screenshot (formula images should be rewritten as LaTeX, not embedded)
`course_memory.json` exists next to the source file
`course_memory.json` records at least one term or symbol introduced by this lecture

evals.name: image-heavy-deck
evals.prompt: 把这个 pptx 整理成讲义。
evals.expected_output: A Chinese study note where decorative images (logos, footer ornaments, repeated branding) have been dropped, while one or more genuinely instructional figures are kept with `> **图解(...)**` explanations.
assertions: Output file `image_heavy_notes.md` exists
Number of `![...](...)` image references in the output is strictly less than the number of image files originally extracted into `image_heavy_assets/`
Every kept image is preceded (within 5 lines above) by a `> **图解(图 X-Y,<role>):**` explanation block
Every `decision: keep` entry in `.ppt2notes_work/image_heavy/image_decisions.json` is embedded in `image_heavy_notes.md`
Allowed `<role>` values in 图解 blocks are one of: chart, formula, diagram, screenshot, photo
No kept image has the role `decoration`
Output contains both `## 复习要点` and `## 思考题`
`course_memory.json` exists next to the source file

evals.name: scanned-pdf-graceful-failure
evals.prompt: 看看这个 pdf,帮我做点笔记。
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that the PDF has no extractable text layer and stops cleanly with an explanation pointing the user toward OCR. No notes file is generated.
assertions: No `scanned_notes.md` file is created next to the source
The agent's reply explicitly mentions one of: 'OCR', 'scanned', 'text layer', or '文字层'
The agent's reply suggests a concrete next step (e.g., naming an OCR tool such as `ocrmypdf`, or asking the user to provide a text-layer PDF)
The agent did NOT silently produce a near-empty notes file
The agent did NOT create or update `course_memory.json` for this failed extraction

evals.name: very-short-deck
evals.prompt: 这是我课上随手记的几张 slides,帮我整理成一份小笔记。
evals.expected_output: A Chinese study note treating the small deck as a single chapter (instead of forcing 3-8 chapters). 复习要点 and 思考题 are still present.
assertions: Output file `tiny_deck_notes.md` exists
Number of `## ` chapter headings (excluding 复习要点 and 思考题) equals exactly 1
Output contains `## 复习要点`
Output contains `## 思考题`
Output is not empty: total length of the markdown body exceeds 200 characters
`course_memory.json` exists next to the source file

evals.name: course-memory-continuity
evals.prompt: 先把第一份课件整理成讲义；之后在新的 agent 会话里，用同一个目录下的 course_memory.json 处理第二份课件。
evals.expected_output: The first run creates course_memory.json. The second run reads it, updates it, and produces concept_followup_zh_notes.md with a concise continuity bridge to the prior lecture.
assertions: After the first run, `course_memory.json` exists next to `concept_heavy_zh.pptx`
After the second run, `concept_followup_zh_notes.md` exists
The second note contains a concise `> **与前文的衔接**` block or equivalent opening bridge
`course_memory.json` contains lecture records for both `concept_heavy_zh.pptx` and `concept_followup_zh.pptx` without duplicate `source_file` entries
`course_memory.json` includes continuity data such as `last_lecture_summary` and non-empty `open_threads`
The second note uses prior terms or concepts consistently without pasting the first note's full content

evals.name: lecture-directory-mode
evals.prompt: 这个 Lec01 文件夹就是一讲，里面有两个 Part PDF、题目、Lab 和一个 C 示例。帮我整理成一份中文讲义。
evals.expected_output: A single Chinese study note named Lec01_notes.md saved in the lecture directory. The run uses lecture directory mode, writes a centralized .ppt2notes_work/Lec01 manifest, keeps course_memory.json at the course root, and incorporates questions/labs/code examples where relevant.
assertions: Output file `Lec01_notes.md` exists in the `Lec01` directory
A centralized `.ppt2notes_work/Lec01/lecture_manifest.json` exists outside the lecture directory
A `.ppt2notes_work/Lec01/coverage_report.json` exists and lists every source file: `01-PartA.pdf`, `02-PartB.pdf`, `Q0.1.pdf`, `Lab01.pdf`, and `scanner_example.c`
The coverage report records page counts or character counts for every source file
`course_memory.json` exists at the course root, not inside `Lec01`
The note's main chapters are organized by topic rather than by raw file names
The question PDF is reflected either in an example, checkpoint, or `思考题` item
The lab PDF is reflected as practical guidance or common pitfalls
The C example is quoted or explained only as a focused snippet, not pasted wholesale
`chapter_plan.json` records `deep_explanation_targets` for central, formula-heavy, algorithmic, or easily confused topics
`chapter_plan.json` and `image_decisions.json` exist in the centralized work directory
